Title: Trehalose-6-phosphate signaling regulates lateral root formation in Arabidopsis thaliana
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGATA23 (tissue-specific in lateral root founder cells); native pTPPB; overexpression promoter NA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: central sugar signal linking carbon/energy status to lateral root formation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: early lateral root primordia / lateral root founder cells in roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TPPB negatively controls T6P levels and suppresses lateral root formation; loss/downregulation increases LR branching density, while overexpression decreases it
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed from stage I onward during lateral root formation; differential expression in lateral root primordia cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early lateral root primordia and lateral root founder cell-associated stages in roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin (NAA) down-regulates TPPB; DMNB-T6P/high-light uncaging and T6P treatments used to probe pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter::GUS expression analysis; T-DNA knockout/knockdown mutants; tissue-specific CRISPR/Cas9 knockout using pGATA23; overexpression lines; LR density and primordia staging; qRT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPPB is the only TPP family member strongly and dynamically expressed during early lateral root formation. 2. tppb mutants and tissue-specific TPPB knockout increase lateral root branching and early primordia density. 3. TPPB overexpression reduces lateral root branching. 4. Auxin represses TPPB through the IAA14/ARF7/ARF19 module, supporting a model where TPPB reduction raises local T6P to promote LR formation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: in silico use of VisuaLRTC transcriptome compendium; lateral root single-cell transcriptome data (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Local suppression of TPPB in root founder cells could be used to enhance root branching and potentially nutrient/water foraging without reducing primary root length; tissue-specific strategies may minimize pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TPPB acts in T6P degradation rather than biosynthesis; although enzymatic, in this study it is central as a metabolic-control gene linking auxin and energy signaling rather than mapped through direct enzyme biochemistry
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No primary root length penalty reported under tested conditions

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes trehalose-6-phosphate from UDP-glucose and glucose-6-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for local T6P production in the transgenic system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: central sugar signal linking carbon/energy status to lateral root formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: lateral root founder cells / early lateral root tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Founder-cell-specific expression of otsA increases local T6P and promotes lateral root branching plus both non-emerged and emerged lateral roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic expression in lateral root founder cells under pGATA23
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: No exogenous treatment required for transgenic effect
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic tissue-specific overexpression; qRT-PCR; LR density and staging analyses; SnRK1 marker expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pGATA23:otsA lines show elevated otsA transcripts in roots. 2. Local TPS activity in LR founder cells increases LR branching density. 3. Both non-emerged primordia and emerged LRs are increased, supporting sufficiency of local T6P biosynthesis for LR induction. 4. SnRK1 marker genes are perturbed consistently with T6P signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Cell-type-specific TPS engineering could enhance root system branching and improve soil exploration; local expression is preferable to avoid whole-plant perturbation of sugar signaling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No primary root length penalty reported under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Heterologous bacterial gene used as a clean gain-of-function tool to elevate T6P locally in planta

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: ABRC CS69182; KINO1 ABRC CS69185; KINO2 ABRC CS69186
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGATA23 for tissue-specific CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in LR founder cells
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: energy signaling and growth suppression during lateral root formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: kinase-mediated signaling; activity is inhibited functionally by T6P
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SnRK1 marker genes including UDPGDH; TPS5; bZIP11; ASN1; TPS8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of ASN1 and TPS8; represses UDPGDH, TPS5, and bZIP11 as inferred from marker-gene behavior
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: KIN11; T6P; RAPTOR1B (interaction referenced from prior work)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: energy-status signal promoting lateral root formation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots, especially lateral root formation zones
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced KIN10/SnRK1 activity promotes lateral root branching, whereas KIN10 overexpression suppresses branching and blocks DMNB-T6P-induced LR promotion
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative regulator whose inhibition is required for T6P-mediated LR induction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Regulated during LR formation and down-regulated in lateral root primordium cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in LRP cells; manipulated in early LR stages via pGATA23-TSKO
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMNB-T6P with high-light uncaging; T6P treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutants; overexpression lines; tissue-specific CRISPR knockout; marker-gene qRT-PCR; LR density phenotyping with/without DMNB-T6P
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. kin10 mutants show increased lateral root density. 2. KIN10 overexpression reduces emerged LR density. 3. Founder-cell-specific KIN10 knockout is sufficient to increase LR formation. 4. T6P treatment changes SnRK1 marker genes consistent with KIN10 pathway inhibition. 5. Elevated KIN10 activity prevents T6P-driven LR induction.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: VisuaLRTC transcriptome compendium; lateral root single-cell transcriptome data (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Moderate cell-specific reduction of SnRK1 activity may be used to increase root branching, but broad suppression could disrupt energy homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects on global energy signaling if altered constitutively; tissue-specific control recommended
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This study emphasizes developmental context distinct from stress-induced LR initiation

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: energy signaling during lateral root formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: kinase-mediated signaling; inhibited functionally by T6P
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: energy-status signal promoting lateral root formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: kin11 mutants display increased lateral root density, supporting SnRK1 as a negative regulator of LR formation downstream of T6P
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes to the negative SnRK1 branch that must be reduced for LR induction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Regulated during LR formation and down-regulated in LRP cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; LR density phenotyping with DMNB-T6P interaction tests
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. kin11 mutants increase LR density. 2. Additional DMNB-T6P does not further elevate branching strongly, supporting a threshold model for SnRK1 reduction in T6P action.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: VisuaLRTC transcriptome compendium; lateral root single-cell transcriptome data (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance branching if modulated locally; broad manipulation may affect carbon-stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible pleiotropic defects in energy/stress signaling if constitutively altered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as functionally overlapping with KIN10 in this developmental context

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGATA23 for tissue-specific CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in LR founder cells
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: growth-promoting energy signaling during lateral root initiation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: kinase-mediated activation of growth programs; positively influenced by T6P
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TOR complex/growth output genes; precise direct targets not resolved here
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: T6P slightly increases TOR phosphorylation and TOR transcript abundance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: TOR complex with RAPTOR1A; RAPTOR1B; LST8
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sugar signal coupling carbon/energy status to organ growth
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root branching zone / early LR cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Local TOR activity is required for LR formation and for T6P-mediated promotion of root branching
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator in the T6P-SnRK1-TOR hub controlling LR biogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TOR-complex genes are more strongly expressed in lateral root primordium cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Early LR stages including LR founder cells
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: T6P treatment; DMNB-T6P plus high light uncaging; sucrose in phosphorylation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: tissue-specific CRISPR knockout; phospho-TOR immunoblot; qRT-PCR; genetic interaction with DMNB-T6P using TOROE and raptor1b mutants; LR phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tissue-specific TOR knockout lowers LR branching density. 2. T6P slightly increases TOR phosphorylation and elevates TOR-complex gene transcripts. 3. T6P cannot promote LR biogenesis when TOR pathway context is altered, indicating dependence on TOR signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: VisuaLRTC transcriptome compendium; lateral root single-cell transcriptome data (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enhancing local TOR activity together with T6P modulation may improve root branching, but strong constitutive activation may disrupt growth balance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad TOR manipulation may cause major pleiotropic growth and metabolic effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper supports a positive relationship between T6P and TOR but mechanism remains partly unresolved

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: TOR-mediated growth signaling during lateral root formation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: TOR complex with TOR and LST8
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TOR; LST8; SnRK1 interaction noted from prior studies
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: energy signal linked to lateral root development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: raptor1b mutants are not responsive to T6P-mediated LR promotion, indicating RAPTOR1B-dependent TOR signaling is required
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required TOR-complex component for T6P-driven LR biogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcriptionally regulated during LR formation and more strongly expressed in LRP cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant interaction test with DMNB-T6P and LR phenotyping; expression analysis of TOR-complex genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAPTOR1B is enriched during LR formation. 2. raptor1b mutants block the promotive effect of T6P on LR branching, placing TOR complex function downstream of T6P.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: VisuaLRTC transcriptome compendium; lateral root single-cell transcriptome data (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning TOR complex assembly may modulate root branching, but broad effects on growth likely require cell-type-specific manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad developmental effects if constitutively perturbed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because genetic interaction with T6P was directly tested in Results

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin-dependent transcriptional control of T6P degradation during LR initiation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression module controlling auxin responses
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TPPB via IAA14/ARF7/ARF19 auxin response module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for auxin-mediated downregulation of TPPB
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: sugar signal connecting auxin to energy balance in LR formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: IAA14/SLR1-dependent auxin signaling represses TPPB, thereby favoring higher T6P and LR induction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream auxin module linking hormone signaling to local T6P accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: auxin treatment followed by qRT-PCR/GUS; mutant response analysis in slr1 background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Auxin strongly represses TPPB expression. 2. This repression is reduced in slr1, indicating dependence on the IAA14 auxin response module.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating auxin-TPPB coupling could tune root branching, though wider auxin pleiotropy is a major consideration
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong pleiotropy expected due to broad auxin developmental roles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of the T6P branch rather than directly on metabolite synthesis

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin-dependent repression of TPPB to increase local T6P signaling during LR formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation within auxin response pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Contributes to auxin-dependent downregulation of TPPB
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: central sugar signal for LR formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ARF7 is required, together with ARF19, for full auxin repression of TPPB and thereby for the auxin-to-T6P connection during LR formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream auxin transcriptional controller of TPPB/T6P module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: auxin response assay in arf7arf19 mutants; promoter motif analysis; cycloheximide test supporting direct regulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Auxin repression of TPPB is abolished in arf7arf19 mutants. 2. ARF7/19-dependent signaling links auxin to increased local T6P during LR initiation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential indirect leverage point for root architecture, but broad developmental functions limit direct engineering utility
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because mutant tests directly establish its role in regulating the metabolically relevant TPPB node

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin-dependent repression of TPPB to support local T6P accumulation during LR formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation within auxin response pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Contributes to auxin-dependent downregulation of TPPB
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: central sugar signal for LR formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ARF19 is required with ARF7 for full auxin repression of TPPB and for the auxin–T6P regulatory connection promoting LR formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream auxin transcriptional controller of TPPB/T6P module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: auxin response assay in arf7arf19 mutants; promoter motif analysis; cycloheximide test
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Auxin-mediated TPPB downregulation requires ARF19 together with ARF7. 2. This establishes a direct auxin-responsive route to increase local T6P signaling in LR cells.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible route to tune root branching indirectly, but broad auxin pleiotropy is a limitation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally paired with ARF7 in this study
